name: Apache Cassandra — 3-node cluster (calibration #7, ScyllaDB bake-off benchmark)

descriptions.stress: cassandra-stress driving the 3-node cluster directly, swept toward the write ceiling. ScyllaDB's own bake-off benchmark: 500 threads, 1.3 TB dataset, replication factor 3, consistency QUORUM. (client.web speaks HTTP in the catalog; here it stands for the CQL-native cassandra-stress client — the numeric capacity path is protocol-agnostic, the same convention the Redis/Kafka/RabbitMQ calibration models use.)
descriptions.cassandra: One Cassandra 3-node cluster (RF=3) modeled as a single SDA node whose aggregate write capacity is the `throughput` cell (op/s); nodeQueues reads it directly for rho, so the calibrated tunable is db.cassandra:throughput — SDA's ESTIMATED single-node 12,800 op/s default, corrected here to the measured 3-node CLUSTER write ceiling (ScyllaDB's bake-off benchmark of Apache Cassandra 3.0.9: 70,771 op/s). connectionPool/connectionHeldMs (64 slots from cassandra.yaml's per-node concurrent_reads + concurrent_writes, held time solved so pool/held reproduces the fitted cluster ceiling) keep the DES queueing tail arithmetically consistent with the scored ceiling — the same consistency rule the Redis entry follows (graph-read.ts finite-pool station); the 3-node coordinator/replica fan-out is not separately decomposed, the same aggregate-cluster simplification the Kafka entry uses for its 3-broker cluster.
